description_long: A cluster of mushrooms is growing from a patch of dark, rich soil. Each mushroom features a rounded, light brown cap with subtle, slightly darker spots, and a smooth, cylindrical white stem. The caps vary in size, with some mushrooms appearing more mature and others still small and developing. The arrangement is natural and slightly irregular, with mushrooms spaced unevenly across the soil patch. The surface of the caps is matte and dotted, giving them a soft, organic appearance. The overall look is reminiscent of edible button mushrooms commonly found in urban parks or gardens.
description: A group of light brown, spotted mushrooms with white stems growing from a dark soil patch. The caps are rounded and matte, with varying sizes and a natural, clustered arrangement.
description_view_0: This view shows the cluster from the front, highlighting the largest mushroom in the center and several smaller mushrooms surrounding it on a dark soil base.
description_view_1: From this angle, the mushrooms appear more staggered, with the rightmost caps more prominent and the smaller mushrooms in the middle clearly visible.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the height differences among the mushrooms, with the tallest stems and caps on the right and a denser grouping on the left.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals fewer mushrooms, with the caps mostly facing away and the soil patch more visible, showing the backs of the largest mushrooms.
